title: The Star Garden
paragraphs: Have you ever wondered where the stars go during the day?
At the very top of the sky, there is an invisible garden. The soil there is deep blue, and the water is made of moonlight dew. During the day, the stars rest in this garden.
Every star is a flower. The brightest ones are the biggest blooms. The little twinkling stars are tiny buds just beginning to sprout.
An old gardener tends the garden. His beard is silver and long, like a little stream. Every day he waters the star-flowers, loosens the soil, and trims their leaves.
In the evening, the old gardener opens the garden gate and lets the star-flowers fly into the sky. One by one they rise up, find their places on the dark canvas, and quietly bloom.
If you look closely, you'll notice each star twinkles at a different rhythm. That's because they're breathing. The big stars breathe slowly and steadily. The small ones breathe a little faster.
Sometimes a star-flower falls from the sky, trailing a long tail behind it. That's not a star dying — it's the old gardener scattering a ripe seed toward the earth.
If you see a shooting star, close your eyes and make a wish. That seed is looking for a warm heart where it can take root.
Now, close your eyes. Perhaps a tiny star-seed is gently landing in your heart, ready to bloom into the most beautiful flower in your dreams. Goodnight.
